body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
This PR makes an initial pass at reworking our docs. Here's an overview of the changes, and a screenshot of the new pages

#### Renamed/moved
- rename: getting started -> running your first simulation
- rename: ClimaLand structure -> repository structure
- move: diagnostics, leaderboard -> analyzing model output
  - combine these into one folder
- move: time type, misc. utilities -> software utilities
  - put into new page
  - rename: timemanager.md -> itime.md
- rename: restarts -> Restarting a Simulation

#### New pages
- new page: julia intro, briefly describing the following:
  - repl, julia environments (project, manifest) and packages, revise
  - and links to helpful resources
- new page: model structure
- new page: Running on GPU or with MPI
- new page: Physical Units
  - moved from landing page

#### Other changes
- restructured landing page
  - add overview of docs pages and what's in each (do this last)
  - move links to end
- add to contributor guide


## To-do
- [x] remove TODOs (add links)
- [x] check that links work
- [x] proofread for typos


## To do in separate PRs (later work) - see https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/issues/1212
- add pages about: 
  - running with prescribed atmospheres
  -  timestepping information, or at least pointer to CTS.jl (maybe timestepping.jl tutorial is sufficient, but it should be more visible)
  - types of boundary conditions available
  - ClimaLand domains, how to set up sims on each
- update page: running your first simulation
  - replace canopy GPP example with example setting up and running EnergyHydrology sim using default parameterizations and plotting, after KMD constructor is merged
- update page: model structure - add information about simulations, once implemented/in use
  - LandSimulation docstring has good info to start with, but we should keep docs consistent with what's currently used in tutorials/experiments
- (other) use .buildkite env for docs too?

We can also consider the structure introduced in https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/pull/1146/files, and add some of the suggestions there if we want to.


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: update docs

body:
## Purpose 
Make model setup easier for soil models forced with atmospheric data. Our current constructor works well for simple cases, but when dealing with spatially varying parameters, and different parameterizations for albedo, etc, this helps expose that things that will change (the parameters, the parameterizations, the domain, the forcing), but hides that the things that will remain the same (how to setup the BC, create the model, sources, etc).

The thought is that this could be followed by a PR enacting something similar for snow, than canopy parameterizations, then canopy, and then land models

## To-do
Better understand how to use this in simpler use cases. What about at a site where we know the soil type (clay) but not the parameters? 

What about if we want to pass scalar parameters for all (that should work as is) - can demo in another case

add roughness parameters?


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->


<!---
Review checklist

I have:

In the Content, I have included

## Purpose 
The branch used in #1211 was based on a old version of main which did not have an important bug fix in the computation of the soil composition parameters. When I rebased, this was lost(and some NaNs reintroduced!). This PR makes our soil spatial parameters match what we had before.


## To-do
Double check that NaNs are gone again


## Content
